description: Trigger discrimination for the WHOLE-design tech spec (this skill) vs ONE decision (adr-writer), the user-facing PRODUCT spec (product-spec-writer), and deciding the system STRUCTURE (architecture-designer).

cases.prompt: Write the design doc for this substantial change: proposed design, data model, APIs, alternatives, migration, observability, testing, and risks — for review before we build.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — a whole-design document with cross-cutting concerns for review is a tech spec. Not one decision and not the user-facing product spec.
cases.reason: Integrating the full technical design of a change into one reviewable doc is this skill's core scope; it composes ADRs and architecture output.

cases.prompt: Record the decision to adopt gRPC over REST for internal calls: context, decision, alternatives, consequences, rollback.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (reverse) — one decision with alternatives/consequences/rollback is an ADR, narrower than a tech spec.
cases.reason: adr-writer records one decision. A tech spec embeds/cites several; it doesn't replace the single-decision record.

cases.prompt: Write the product spec: the user problem, goals and non-goals, scenarios, and testable acceptance criteria.
cases.reason: product-spec-writer captures the user-facing what/why and acceptance. The tech spec is the engineering design that satisfies it.

cases.prompt: Decide how this system should be structured: the components, their dependencies, data ownership, and the tradeoffs.
cases.reason: architecture-designer decides the system structure and tradeoffs. The tech spec presents/consumes that result; it doesn't re-derive the structure.
